steps.text: Initiate and develop proposed NPIs within the scope of their authority or significantly affecting the operations of their programs.
Request review and clearance by appropriate directorates, regional offices, Office of the Solicitor (SOL), the Assistant Secretary, etc.
Provide proposed NPI and necessary background information to ensure the proposed issuance is thoroughly understood and considered.
Establish a due date for response return, allowing no more than three weeks turnaround time.
Review and consider comments received and address them as appropriate.

Refer to the appropriate Deputy Assistant Secretary any unsolved disagreements or issues concerning the substance or potential policy implications of the proposed NPI or to the priority accorded to the proposed NPI’s review that emerged during the clearance process.
Ensure that systems for receiving, reviewing, tracking, concurring, and issuing NPIs are effective for consistently complying with requirements of this instruction.
Consider each proposed NPI (and any associated critical policy or non-policy issues) and promptly respond to the originator.
Specify the official authorized to approve the release of the NPI.
Specify the official responsible for maintaining records documenting the views and clearance of each clearance official. Files must be maintained in accordance with OSHA file retention policies.
Specify the official responsible for maintaining and updating these written procedures.
Provide the Director, Directorate of Administrative Programs, a copy of the procedure and any future amendments.
Submit NPIs to the web administrator by the directorate or regional web officer for the issuing directorate, region, or office. All NPIs must be 508 compliant before submission for publication.
Review, approve, and sign the OSHA 201 before distribution to the clearance officials.
Review comments and originator’s recommendations and approves the final draft directive.
Elevate disputes with clearance officials to the appropriate Deputy Assistant Secretary for resolution.
Notify affected parties when the approved directive is posted on the directives webpage as necessary.
Review the draft directive. If necessary, request an extension from the originator as soon as possible.
Complete item number 9 and 10 of the OSHA 201.
Return the OSHA 201 with comments to the originator. Alternatively, sends concurrence or comments electronically (e.g., email, shared folders, SharePoint, etc.).
Negotiate the acceptance of comments with the originator or originating Program Director or presents the case to the appropriate Deputy Assistant Secretary for resolution.
Review the directive clearance packet to determine if complete and accurate for approval clearance.
Provide revisions request from the Office of the Assistant Secretary (OAS) to the originator for necessary updates.
Provide notification of signed directive to the originator and requests the directive be formatted to a 508-compliant PDF file.
Coordinate union review through the Labor Management Relations Officer.
Assign an effective date based on the approval by the union, delayed posting request, or pending press release.
Provide the final 508-complaint PDF file directive to the web administrator for posting to the Directives webpage.
Review 508-compliant PDF file for web-related technical content.
Publish508-compliant PDF file to OSHA Directives webpage within the requested timeframe.
Notifythe directives officer of availability on the OSHA Directives webpage.
Prepare a cancellation notice using the cancellation template on the OSHA Directives Tools webpage.
Obtain the approval of their Program Director.
Provide the cancellation notice and concurrence grid providing review by the originating directorate, the directives officer, and the OAS.
Provide the final unsigned 508-compliant PDF file to the directives officer for posting to the directives webpage.
Review the cancellation packet to determine if complete and accurate for the approval process.
Provide revision request from the OAS to the originator for necessary updates.
Provide notification of signed directive to the originator and request the cancellation notice be formatted to 508-compliant PDF.
Assign an effective date based on the approval by the Assistant Secretary.
Provide the final 508-compliant PDF file to the web administrator for posting to the OSHA Directives webpage.
Publish 508-compliant PDF to OSHA Directives webpage within requested timeframe.
Implement and comply with the provisions of this instruction and with related instructions governing the management of policy and non-policy issues.
Self-assess and routinely monitor the directive process using the checklist on the directives tools webpage to ensure compliance with the provisions of the instruction.
Review and assess their active directives on an annual basis with a final document of the review findings maintained in the national office or regional files.
Conduct a full assessment of the files for the directives approved that year and ensure that all appropriate documents are included in the file.
Send an email or memo documenting the completion of the requirement no later than September 30 of the fiscal year.
Retain completion documentation in the directive file record.
The program office should retain completion documentation in the directive file record.
Confirm the self-assessment documentation was received within the established deadline.
Include the auditing of the assessment process as part of its audit responsibilities.
Submit any webpage updates to the directives officer.
Review the OSHA Directives webpage biennially for accuracy.
Provide notification to national office directors and regional administrators of expiring directives.
Archive directives that are canceled by reference in a new or revised instruction, notice, or direction.
Ensure that the OSHA Notice for directive cancellation remains on the active publications page for 90 days.

preconditions.text: Prior to clearance review confirm the current directive template is being used.
